title: Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones (novelization)
content:
{{Top|rwm|imp|title=''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones''}} {{Otheruses|Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones}} {{Book |image=[[File:Attack_of_the_Clones_Cover.jpg]] |title=''Star Wars'': Episode II
''Attack of the Clones'' |author=[[R. A. Salvatore]] |cover artist=[[Steven D. Anderson]] (Original art), [[David Stevenson]] (Yoda art) |illustrator= |editor= |publisher= *[[Del Rey]] (US) *[[Arrow Books]] (UK) |early release= |release date=[[April 23]], [[2002]]{{RandomHouseOld|url=author/results.pperl?authorid=26771|text=R.A. Salvatore}} |intl release= |rereleased= |media type=*Hardcover *Paperback{{PenguinRandomHouse|url=books/160114/attack-of-the-clones-star-wars-episode-ii-by-r-a-salvatore|text=Attack of the Clones: Star Wars: Episode II}} |pages=336 |isbn=0345428811 |isbn2= |isbn3= |timeline=[[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' |series=Film novelizations |preceded by=''[[The Approaching Storm]]'' |followed by=''[[Star Wars Adventures 1: Hunt the Sun Runner]]'' }} The '''''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones''''' novelization by [[R. A. Salvatore]], published by [[Del Rey]] on [[April 23]], [[2002]], is the official novel adaptation of the film [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']]. It includes expansion of scenes from the movie, many scenes cut from the movie, and several original scenes. The paperback edition includes 32 pages of storyboard art from the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|First Battle of Geonosis]], an introduction by [[Rick McCallum]], and a preview of [[Matthew Stover]]'s novel [[Shatterpoint (novel)|''Shatterpoint'']]. Narration for the audio version was performed by [[Jonathan Davis]]. ==Publisher's summary== ===Hardcover=== There is a great disturbance in the Force…. ''From the sleek ships of the glimmering Coruscant skyscape to the lush gardens of pastoral Naboo, dissent is roiling. The Republic is failing, even under the leadership of Supreme Chancellor Palpatine, elected ten years earlier to save the crumbling government. Separatists threate war, and the Senate is hopelessly divided, unable to determine whether to raise an army for battle or keep the fragile peace. It is a stalemate that, once broken, could lead to galactic chaos.'' ''Mischievous and resolved, courageous to the point of recklessness, Anakin Skywalker has come of age in a time of great upheaval. The nineteen-year-old apprentice to Obi-Wan Kenobi is an enigma to the Jedi Council, and a challenge to his Jedi Master. Time has not dulled Anakin's ambition, nor has his Jedi training tamed his independent streak. When an attempt on Senator Padmé Amidala's life brings them together for the first time in ten years, it is clear that time also has not dulled Anakin's intense feelings for the beautiful diplomat.'' ''The attack on Senator Amidala just before a crucial vote thrusts the Republic even closer to the edge of disaster. Masters Yoda and Mace Windu sense enormous unease. The dark side is growing, clouding the Jedi's perception of the events. Unbeknownst to the Jedi, a slow rumble is building into the roar of thousands of soldiers readying for battle. But even as the Republic falters around them, Anakin and Padmé find a connection so intense that all else begins to fall away. Anakin will lose himself—and his way—in emotions a Jedi, sworn to hold allegiance only to the Order, is forbidden to have.'' ===Paperback=== ''Courageous to the point of recklessness, and possessing a wild independent streak, twenty-year-old Anakin Skywalker, apprentice to Obi-Wan Kenobi, is coming of age in a time of great upheaval. The attempted assassination of Senator Padmé Amidala before a crucial vote thrusts the Republic even closer to the edge of disaster. Unbeknownst to the Jedi, a low rumble of discontent is building into the roar of thousands of clone soldiers readying for the ultimate fight. The dark side is growing. Masters Yoda and Mace Windu sense enormous unease…for the fiercest battle of dark versus light is fast approaching, threatening to consume them all in a tidal wave of war.'' ==Plot summary== {{Plot-link|Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones}} ==Continuity== ===Differences between the movie and the novel=== *[[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin's]] relationship is explored in greater detail. *On Naboo, Anakin and Padmé playfully wrestle after Anakin pulls a joke on her. In the movie, though Anakin has the superior strength, Padmé's beauty, strength, and more focused combat skill give her the advantage. After a brief tussle, she defeats him and smiles. In the novel, Anakin bests Padmé with his superior strength. *The [[Lars family/Legends|Lars family]] is introduced, and has its own subplot leading up to when they appear in the movie. The novel reveals how [[Cliegg Lars/Legends|Cliegg Lars]] lost his leg and deepens the relationship between [[Shmi Skywalker Lars/Legends|Shmi Skywalker]], [[Owen Lars/Legends|Owen Lars]], and [[Beru Whitesun Lars/Legends|Beru Whitesun]]. *Anakin's childhood episode with sparing the life of a wounded [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raider]] in the [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (novelization)|Episode I novel]] by [[Terry Brooks]] (which did not appear in [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|the film]]) was briefly referenced in the Episode II novel (which likewise didn't surface in the Episode II film). *During the battle in the [[Petranaki arena]], the [[Nexu/Legends|nexu]] that attacks Padmé survives longer than in the film, pursuing her as she rides the [[Reek/Legends|reek]] alongside Anakin, and then attacking the spear-wounded [[Acklay/Legends|acklay]]. Although it is mentioned that all three of the execution monsters die in the Jedi-droid battle, the acklay's death is the only one described—it is killed by [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan]], as in the film. *During Anakin's duel with Count Dooku on Geonosis, Anakin briefly uses his green lightsaber as well as the blue one given to him by Obi-Wan. The film depicts Count Dooku slashing in half Anakin's green blade, forcing him to using his blue lightsaber, whereas the novel describes Count Dooku cutting away Anakin's blue blade, forcing him to use his green lightsaber. ==Media== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones (novelization)}} ===Editions=== ====US==== *{{ISBN|0345428811}}; [[April 23]],
